core_mechanism: resonant nonlinear mode-coupling (four-wave mixing) that redistributes energy among discrete frequency modes of a modulationally unstable uniform background, pumping amplitude from linearly growing (unstable) modes into a linearly stable mode until that stable mode dominates
brief_reason: The abstract attributes the spontaneous, seedless amplification of a linearly stable harmonic to four-wave mixing accounting for 96% of the nonlinear forcing, acting on unstable-mode perturbations of a continuous-wave background within a modulation-instability gain band.
